content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs CornrMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramenro, CA g5gl1_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 20, 2010
Sachi A. Hamai
Executive Officer
County of Los Angeles
Board of Supervisors
500 West Temple Street, Room 383
Los Angeles, CA 9OOl2
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-091
Dear Ms. Hamai:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding statement-of-economic interest
disclosure under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 ?ou. ,"qrr"st is made on behalf of the
County of Los Angeles Board of Supervisors. The advice of the Fair political practices
Commission (the "Commission") is limited to matters arising under the Act. (Regulation
1832e(b)(8)(d).)
QUESTTONS
l. Are board members of Vah-re Schools, a nonprofit public benefit corporation
that operates two charter schools created by authorization of the Los Angeles unified School
District ("LAUSD"), "public officials"? If they are public officials, muJ they file statements of
economic interests?
2. If the answers to the foregoing questions are "yes," where should the board
mernbers file?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Yes. Members of the board of Value Schools are public officials and must
file statements of economic interests in accordance with the applicable conflict-of'-interest code.
| 'Ihe Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections ,S 1000 through 9l0l.l. All statutory
ret'erencesaretotheGovernmentCode,unless0therwiseindicated. Theregulations()ftheF;irpoliticat practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through ltt997 of Title 2 of the california Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ref-erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless,therwise indicated.


File No. A-10-091
Page No. 2
2. Because the charter schools operated by Value Schools were authorized by
LAUSD and the LAUSD conflict-of-interest code includes a disclosure category that covers the
roles of the board members, the board members must file statements of economic interests in the
place designated by the LAUSD conflict-of-interest code.
FACTS
The Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors serves as the code reviewing body for any
local government agency, other than a city agency, with jurisdiction within the county oi Lo,
Angeles. This includes LAUSD,2 Two 
"hort"r sciools,6"ntrol City Value School and
Downtown Value School, (collectively, the "Charter Schools") werl created by authorization of
LAUSD. The Charter Schools are located within Los Angeles County. They are operated by
Value Schools, a non-profit public benefit corporation.
Value Schools and each charter school have a board of directors. All of the board
members of Value Schools also serve as board members of the Charter Schools, in accordance
with the "Policies on School Governance" of value Schools.
The Charter Schools do not have a conflict-of-interest code. LAUSD has a conflict-of-
interest code that includes a disclosure category for "persons who perform under a charter
agreement the duties of any position similar to that of any designaied position" on the LAUSD
confl ict-of-interest code.
ANALYSIS
Section 87301 provides that it is the policy of the Act that conflict-of-interest codes shall
be formulated at the most decentralized level possible. You have stated that the County Board of
Strpervisors is the code-reviewing body for LAUSD. Ordinaril y, any question regarding the
applicability of a conflict-of-interest code to a particular person or position is made by tle code-
reviewing body. However, you advise that the County B-oard of Supervisors has been unable to
determine whether board members of Value Schools ire subject to the LAUSD conflict-of-
interest code and you are asking our advice on behalf of the bounty Board of Supervisors.
Under these circumstances, we will address your questions.
Ouestion l: Are Value Schools' board members "public fficicils" under the Act? If they
are public fficials, must theyJile stutements of economic interests?
The Commission's longstanding advice has been that charter schools organized under
Education Code section47600 et seq. are local government agencies and that m-embers of a
charter school's board of directors are "public officials." (FadelyAdvice Letter, No. A-OZ-223;
Walsh Advice Letter, No. 4-98-234). The Charter Schools are organized under this provision of
' LAUSD encompasses the western section of Los Angeles County


File No. A-10-091
Page No. 3
the Education Code and are, therefore, required to either adopt a conflict-of-interest-code or be
incolporated in the conflict-of-interest code of their authorizingagency. (Fadellt, supra).
The "Policies on School Governance" of Value Schools states that "the directors on the
board of directors of Value Schools shall be the directors on the board of directors for each of its
schools." Accordingly, the board members of Value Schools, who are also board members of
each charter school, must file statements of economic interests in accordance with the applicable
confl ict-of- interest code.
Ouestion 2: Wltot is tlrc opplicctble conflict-of-interest code that determines where the boctrd
members must file?
You have indicated that the two charter schools have not adopted a conflict-of-interest
code. LAUSD has a conflict-of-interest code that, with your permission as a supplement to the
facts you present, we have obtained from the LAUSD website.
The conflict-of-interest code of LAUSD includes a disclosure category for "persons who
perform under a charter agreement the duties of any position similar to thai of ury designated
position on the LAUSD conflict-of-interest code . . .." This category is referenced in the list of
designated positions as "Charter School Providers" under the title "Non-Employee Filers."
We have reviewed the LAUSD conflict-of-interest code and the internal documents of
Value School that you have submitted describing the responsibilities of Value Schools'board
members. It is clear that the board of directors of Value Schools perform duties of positions
similar to those of designated positions in the LAUSD conflicrof-interest code. These include
the adoption of by-laws of the Charter Schools. Principals of the Charter Schools are required
to: hire, supervise and evaluate school personnel subject to the policies of Value Schooli;
operate the Charter Schools subiect to the curriculum stondards and program assessment for
Value Schools; obtain the approvol of Value Schools for facility impiovernents, obtain approval
of budgets from the Value Schools board of directors, prepare school budgets according to the
budget preparation guidelines of Volue Schools: obtain the approvat of tie boctrd of diiectors of
Value Schools for annual operating budgets, capital budgets and salaries and benefits of all full-
time, permanent employees of the Charter Schools. The board of directors of Value Schools
selects the auditor(s) for an annual audit of the Charter Schools and approves certain budget
alterations, contracts, leases and purchases by the Charter schools. 3
Under the foregoing Value School policies and "regulations," the Value Schools board
members appear to perform under a charter agreement the duties of positions similar to those of
designated positions in the LAUSD conflict-of-interest code and are, therefore, subject to the
conflict-of-interest code of LAUSD. The positions we have noted are: Superintendent of
Schools, Chief Human Resources Officer, Director - Salary Allocation, Supervising purchasing
Services Coordinator, Director of Contracts Administration, Chief Academic Officer and Chiei
Financial Officer.
r These provisions are set forth in the "Policies on School Governance," "policies on Financial
Management and Operations" and "Regulations on Financial Management and Operations" of Value Schools


File No. A-10-091
Page No.4
Because the directors are subject to the LAUSD conflict-of-interest code, they must file
their statements of economic interests in the place designated in that code.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
VJ:jgl
